reasoning: AT&T closed at a local high of 25.99 on 2025-02-28 (up sharply from ~25.0 in late Feb). The short-term statistical baseline given by yfinance predicts P(>25.99) = 0.40 since 25.99 is at the very top of recent bounds. The trend is positive though, with analyst upgrades (up to $32) and solid Q4 fundamentals driving outperformance. For the very near term (March 9), I put it slightly below 0.5 (0.46) due to the possibility of a minor mean reversion from the 1-week pop, aligned slightly above the yfinance baseline of 0.40 because of the fundamental momentum (as indicated by SeekingAlpha on Feb 28). Going out further, T has a positive expected drift via its turnaround, debt reduction, and higher price targets. However, as it pays ~4.6% dividend yield (~28 cents/quarter), expected returns are partly converted to cash reducing price. Thus, over months, the upward price drift might be slower than the overall return. The dates span out incrementally: for 2025-04-01, I give it 0.51 as fundamental momentum counters the reversion. For 2025-05-31 and 2025-08-29, the long-term trend makes >25.99 more likely, but we have headwinds of upcoming ex-dividend drops (likely in April and July), limiting the upward probability, pushing it to 0.54 and 0.58. By 2026-03-02, general stock market risk premium and T's target of $26-$32 makes it fairly likely to be strictly higher than $25.99, although total returns are more important for T than purely stock price. I estimate 0.66 for a year out. Uncertainty increases over time, and a broader window of possible outcomes supports bringing distant probabilities somewhat toward more moderate conviction levels, while letting underlying risk premiums lift them above 0.50.
